spec.title: Helper result schema and redacted validation

humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers after helper-result schema and secretRef component validation repairs. One P3 diagnostic-noise item is recorded for later cleanup.

findings.title: Invalid helper-result fixture reports extra redaction diagnostic
findings.description: The invalid base64url path-component fixture correctly reports secretRefs.0.secretRef, but also reports diagnostic.message:kaifuu.secret_redacted because the synthetic diagnostic message contains secretRef wording.

evidence.detail: Re-audit confirmed the required fixtureId and secretRefs.0.secretRef failure are present and raw material is redacted; the extra redaction diagnostic is safe but noisy.

findings.impact: Invalid fixture reports are safe, but the extra redaction failure can distract from the primary helper-result validation error.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The invalid base64url path-component helper-result fixture still fails on secretRefs.0.secretRef.
The fixture or validator avoids a redundant diagnostic.message redaction failure when the primary secretRef error is already emitted.
Raw secret-like material remains redacted from reports and CLI output.

proposedDagNode.title: Helper-result invalid fixture diagnostic noise
proposedDagNode.summary: Reduce redundant redaction diagnostics for helper-result invalid fixtures while preserving strict secretRef failures and redaction.
deliverables: CLI report assertion for primary secretRef failure without redundant message noise
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid base64url path-component helper-result fixtures fail on secretRefs.0.secretRef.
Reports avoid redundant diagnostic.message redaction failures when the message is synthetic and non-sensitive.
Raw secret-like material remains redacted from reports and CLI output.
